calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-27-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Default Judgment Prove-Up Hearing As To Defendants Mc1 Auto Sales, Llc And Merardo Cera (Notices)
Off Calendar Feb-08-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for default judgment prove-up hearing.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 09:35:29 a.m. and concluding at 10:11:14 a.m.

Plaintiff Orion First Financial, L.L.C. a Washington Limited Liability Company represented by Mark M. Scott, Esq.
Defendant MCL Auto Sales, LLC, a New Mexico Limited Liability Company not appearing.
Defendant Merardo Cera not appearing.

Matthew Jacobs (Senior Litigation Paralegal/Custodian of Records) is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

Court has reviewed the case and all filings pertaining to this hearing.
Counsel for Plaintiff presents outline and summary of case.

The following exhibits are MARKED/ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE:

Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 1 - Equipment Financing Agreement
Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 2 - Acceptance Certificate
Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 3 - Personal Guaranty
Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 4 - Collection Agency Agreement
Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 5 - Contract Payment History
Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 6 - Demand Letter
Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 7 - Interest Accrual

Court examines Plaintiff's Counsel.
MATTHEW JACOBS is examined by the Court. Witness attests that statements made by its counsel are true and correct.

The matter is submitted.
Based on the summary of the case, comments made by Plaintiff's Counsel, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has proven each essential element of the complaint and Defendant is in breach of equipment finance agreement.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Orion First Financial, L.L.C. a Washington Limited Liability Company, and against MCL Auto Sales, LLC, a New Mexico Limited Liability Company and Merardo Cera, as follows:

1. Damages: $21,451.98
2. Attorney Fees: $1,638.53
3. Costs: $710.00

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $23,800.51

Proposed judgment signed and filed as modified.
